name: GLSL MAT
description: The GLSL MAT allows you to write or import custom materials into TouchDesigner. When there are compile errors in a GLSL shader, a blue/red checkerboard error shader will be displayed.
summary: The GLSL MAT allows you to write or import custom materials into TouchDesigner. When there are compile errors in a GLSL shader, a blue/red checkerboard error shader will be displayed.

parameters.name: GLSL Version
parameters.label: GLSL Version
parameters.description: Pick what version of GLSL to compile the shader with.
glsl120 - glsl330 - glsl400 - glsl410 - glsl420 - glsl430 - glsl440 - glsl450 - glsl460 -

parameters.name: Preprocess Directives
parameters.label: Preprocess Directives
parameters.description: predat - Use this  to place preprocessor directives at the start of your shader, such as #extension. This is required since these need to be the first lines in the shader, and TouchDesigner will be adding code to the start of your shader to declare uniforms/functions which would appear before #extension directives located in the main shader.

parameters.name: Vertex Shader
parameters.label: Vertex Shader
parameters.description: vdat -  to the  that holds the vertex shader code.

parameters.name: Pixel Shader
parameters.label: Pixel Shader
parameters.description: pdat -  to the  that holds the pixel shader code.

parameters.name: Load Uniform Names
parameters.label: Load Uniform Names
parameters.description: loaduniformnames - This will read all the declared (and used) uniforms in the compiled GLSL shader, and fill in the various name fields for the uniform parameters.

parameters.name: Clear Uniform Names
parameters.label: Clear Uniform Names
parameters.description: clearuniformnames - This will clear all of the name fields for the uniform parameters.

parameters.name: Geometry Shader
parameters.label: Geometry Shader
parameters.description: gdat -  to the  that holds the geometry shader code.

parameters.name: Inherit Uniforms/Samplers from
parameters.label: Inherit Uniforms/Samplers from
parameters.description: inherit - This  will inherit all of the Textures and Uniforms from the GLSL  referenced in this parameter.

parameters.name: Lighting Space
parameters.label: Lighting Space
parameters.description:
lightingspace -  - Allows lighting space switch from the current default World Space to legacy Camera Space which was used for TouchDesigner 088.

 worldspace - cameraspace -

parameters.name: World Space
parameters.label: World Space
parameters.description: worldspace - cameraspace -

parameters.name: Input Primitive Type
parameters.label: Input Primitive Type
parameters.description:
inprim -  - The type of geometry that will be inputed into the Geometry .

 points - lines - triangles -

parameters.name: Points
parameters.label: Points
parameters.description: points - lines - triangles -

parameters.name: Output Primitive Type
parameters.label: Output Primitive Type
parameters.description:
outprim -  - The type of geometry that the Geometry  will output.

 points - linestrip - tristrip -

parameters.name: Num Output Vertices
parameters.label: Num Output Vertices
parameters.description: numout - The maximum number of vertices the Geometry  will output.

parameters.name: Two Sided Coloring
parameters.label: Two Sided Coloring
parameters.description: twocolor - Enables support for two-sided coloring. When this is enabled the  and/or Geometry shader can write to gl_FrontColor, gl_BackColor, gl_FrontSecondaryColor and gl_BackSecondaryColor and the correct color will be placed into gl_Color and gl_SecondaryColor in the Pixel shader depending on if the primitive's front face or back face is facing the camera. When this is disabled the values placed into gl_FrontColor and gl_FrontSecondaryColor are passed to gl_Color and gl_SecondaryColor regardless of which side of the primitive is facing the camera.

parameters.name: Attribute
parameters.label: Attribute
parameters.description: attr - A sequence of attributes that should be made accesible in the shader. In the past these would be manually declared inside the shader, but to add support for POP based workflows, they need to be setup via these parameters instead. You can access the shaders using TDAttrib_ (). attr0name - The name to give the attribute. attr0type - - The type, both vector size as well as if it's a float, double (d), integer (i), or unsigned integer (u). float - vec2 - vec3 - vec4 - double - dvec2 - dvec3 - dvec4 - int - ivec2 - ivec3 - ivec4 - uint - uvec2 - uvec3 - uvec4 - attr0size - The size of the array. Currently a Size == 1 means it's not an array.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.label: Name
parameters.description: attr0name - The name to give the attribute.

parameters.name: Type
parameters.label: Type
parameters.description:
attr0type -  - The type, both vector size as well as if it's a float, double (d), integer (i), or unsigned integer (u).

 float - vec2 - vec3 - vec4 - double - dvec2 - dvec3 - dvec4 - int - ivec2 - ivec3 - ivec4 - uint - uvec2 - uvec3 - uvec4 -

parameters.label: float
parameters.description: float - vec2 - vec3 - vec4 - double - dvec2 - dvec3 - dvec4 - int - ivec2 - ivec3 - ivec4 - uint - uvec2 - uvec3 - uvec4 -

parameters.name: Array Size
parameters.label: Array Size
parameters.description: attr0size - The size of the array. Currently a Size == 1 means it's not an array.

parameters.name: Sampler
parameters.label: Sampler
parameters.description:
sampler - Sequence of samplers for use in the shader



  sampler0name - This is the sampler name that the GLSL program will use to sample from this . The samplers need to be declared at the same dimentions as the  (sampler2D for a 2D , sampler3D for 3D ).



  sampler0top -  - This is the  that will be referenced by the above sampler name above it.	
Exposed by the + Button, texture sampling parameters:				
Refer to the Texture Sampling Parameters article for more information on the parameters exposed by pressing the + button. The parameter prefix for each of the parameters is top[digit].


  sampler0extendu -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  sampler0extendv -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  sampler0extendw -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  sampler0filter -  - 

 nearest - linear - mipmaplinear -

  sampler0anisotropy -  - 

 off - 2x - 4x - 8x - 16x -

parameters.name: TOP
parameters.label: TOP
parameters.description:
sampler0top -  - This is the  that will be referenced by the above sampler name above it.	
Exposed by the + Button, texture sampling parameters:				
Refer to the Texture Sampling Parameters article for more information on the parameters exposed by pressing the + button. The parameter prefix for each of the parameters is top[digit].


  sampler0extendu -  - 

 hold - zero - repeat - mirror -

  sampler0extendv -  -

  sampler0filter -  - 

 nearest - linear - mipmaplinear -

  sampler0anisotropy -  - 

 off - 2x - 4x - 8x - 16x -

parameters.name: Vector
parameters.label: Vector
parameters.description:
vec - Sequence of vectors to be used as uniforms



  vec0name - The name of the vector.



  vec0value -  - The value to assign to the uniform. If the uniform is a float the first entry of the four is used, if the uniform is a vec2 the first two entries are used, etc.

 vec0valuex - vec0valuey - vec0valuez - vec0valuew -

parameters.name: Value
parameters.label: Value
parameters.description:
vec0value -  - The value to assign to the uniform. If the uniform is a float the first entry of the four is used, if the uniform is a vec2 the first two entries are used, etc.

 vec0valuex - vec0valuey - vec0valuez - vec0valuew -

parameters.name: Array
parameters.label: Array
parameters.description: array - Sequence of arrays to be used as uniforms

parameters.name: Matrix
parameters.label: Matrix
parameters.description:
matrix - Sequence of matrices to be used as uniforms



  matrix0name - The name of the uniform. The uniform should be declared as a mat4.



  matrix0value - The value to assign the matrix. For valid ways to specify this, see the Matrix Parameters article.

parameters.name: Relative
parameters.label: Relative
parameters.description:
rel - A sequence of relative transforms. The transform is the relative transform one Object  to another.



  rel0name - The name of the uniform. The uniform should be declared as a mat4.



  rel0from - Transform from this COMPs world transform.



  rel0to - Transform to this COMPs world transform.

parameters.name: Xform from
parameters.label: Xform from
parameters.description: rel0from - Transform from this COMPs world transform.

parameters.name: To
parameters.label: To
parameters.description: rel0to - Transform to this COMPs world transform.

parameters.name: Constant
parameters.label: Constant
parameters.description:
const - Sequence of constant uniforms.



  const0name - The constant name, as declared in the shader.



  const0value - The value to give the constant.

parameters.name: Deform
parameters.label: Deform
parameters.description: dodeform - Enables deforms on this material.

parameters.name: Get Bone Data:
parameters.label: Get Bone Data:
parameters.description:
deformdata -  - Specifies where the deform bone data will be obtained.

 sop - mat - deformin -

parameters.name: From a SOP
parameters.label: From a SOP
parameters.description: sop - mat - deformin -

parameters.name: SOP with Capture Data
parameters.label: SOP with Capture Data
parameters.description: targetsop - Specifies the  that contains the deform capture attributes.

parameters.name: pCaptPath Attrib
parameters.label: pCaptPath Attrib
parameters.description: pcaptpath - Specifies the name of the pCaptPath attribute to use. When your geometry has been put through a Bone Group SOP, the attributes will be split into names like pCaptPath0, pCaptPath1. You can only render 1 bone group at a time, so this should match the group you are rendering with this material.

parameters.name: pCaptData Attrib
parameters.label: pCaptData Attrib
parameters.description: pcaptdata - Much like pCaptPath Attrib.

parameters.name: Skeleton Root Path
parameters.label: Skeleton Root Path
parameters.description: skelrootpath - Specifies the path to the  where the root of the skeleton is located.

parameters.name: MAT
parameters.label: MAT
parameters.description: mat - When obtaining deform data from a  or a Deform In , this is where that  is specified.

parameters.name: Blending (Transparency)
parameters.label: Blending (Transparency)
parameters.description: blending - This toggle enables and disables blending. However see the wiki article Transparency.

parameters.name: Blend Operation
parameters.label: Blend Operation
parameters.description:
blendop -  - 

 add - subtract - revsubtract - minimum - maximum -

parameters.label: Add
parameters.description: add - subtract - revsubtract - minimum - maximum -

parameters.label: Source Color *
parameters.description:
srcblend -  - This value is multiplied by the color value of the pixel that is being written to the Color-Buffer (also know as the Source Color).

 zero - dcol - omdcol - sa - omsa - da - omda - sas - one - constantcol - omconstantcol - constanta - omconstanta -

parameters.label: Zero
parameters.description: zero - dcol - omdcol - sa - omsa - da - omda - sas - one - constantcol - omconstantcol - constanta - omconstanta -

parameters.label: Destination Color *
parameters.description:
destblend -  - This value is multiplied by the color value of the pixel currently in the Color-Buffer (also known as the Destination Color).

 one - scol - omscol - sa - omsa - da - omda - zero - dcol - omdcol - sas - constantcol - omconstantcol - constanta - omconstanta -

parameters.label: One
parameters.description: one - scol - omscol - sa - omsa - da - omda - zero - dcol - omdcol - sas - constantcol - omconstantcol - constanta - omconstanta -

parameters.name: Separate Alpha Function
parameters.label: Separate Alpha Function
parameters.description: separatealphafunc - This toggle enables and disables separate blending options for the alpha values.

parameters.name: Alpha Blend Operation
parameters.label: Alpha Blend Operation
parameters.description:
blendopa -  - 

 add - subtract - revsubtract - minimum - maximum -

parameters.label: Source Alpha *
parameters.description:
srcblenda -  - This value is multiplied by the alpha value of the pixel that is being written to the Color-Buffer (also know as the Source Alpha).

 zero - dcol - omdcol - sa - omsa - da - omda - sas - one - constantcol - omconstantcol - constanta - omconstanta -

parameters.label: Destination Alpha *
parameters.description:
destblenda -  - This value is multiplied by the alpha value of the pixel currently in the Color-Buffer (also known as the Destination Alpha).

 one - scol - omscol - sa - omsa - da - omda - zero - dcol - omdcol - sas - constantcol - omconstantcol - constanta - omconstanta -

parameters.label: Blend Constant Color
parameters.description:
blendconstant -  - 

 blendconstantr - blendconstantg - blendconstantb -

parameters.label: Blend Constant Alpha
parameters.description: blendconstanta -

parameters.name: Legacy Alpha Behavior
parameters.label: Legacy Alpha Behavior
parameters.description: legacyalphabehavior -

parameters.name: Post-Mult Color by Alpha
parameters.label: Post-Mult Color by Alpha
parameters.description: postmultalpha - Multiplies the color by alpha after all other operations have taken place.

parameters.name: Point Color Pre-Multiply
parameters.label: Point Color Pre-Multiply
parameters.description:
pointcolorpremult -  - 

 alreadypremult - premultinshader -

parameters.name: Already Pre-Multiplied By Alpha
parameters.label: Already Pre-Multiplied By Alpha
parameters.description: alreadypremult - premultinshader -

parameters.name: Depth Test
parameters.label: Depth Test
parameters.description: depthtest - Enables and disables the Depth-Test. If the depth-test is disabled, depths values aren't written to the Depth-Buffer.

parameters.name: Depth Test Function
parameters.label: Depth Test Function
parameters.description:
depthfunc -  - The depth value of the pixel being drawn is compared to the depth value currently in the depth-buffer using this function. If the test passes then the pixel is drawn to the -Buffer. If the test fails the pixel is discarded and no changes are made to the -Buffer.

 less - lessorequal - equal - greater - greaterorequal - notequal - always -

parameters.name: Less Than
parameters.label: Less Than
parameters.description: less - lessorequal - equal - greater - greaterorequal - notequal - always -

parameters.name: Write Depth Values
parameters.label: Write Depth Values
parameters.description: depthwriting - If Write Depth Values is on, pixels that pass the depth-test will write their depth value to the Depth-Buffer. If this isn't on then no changes will be made to the Depth-Buffer, regardless of if the pixels drawn pass or fail the depth-test.

parameters.name: Discard Pixels Based on Alpha
parameters.label: Discard Pixels Based on Alpha
parameters.description: alphatest - This enables or disables the pixel alpha test.

parameters.name: Keep Pixels with Alpha
parameters.label: Keep Pixels with Alpha
parameters.description:
alphafunc -  - This menu works in conjunction with the Alpha Threshold parameter below in determining which pixels to keep based on their alpha value.

 less - lessorequal - greater - greaterorequal -

parameters.name: Alpha Threshold
parameters.label: Alpha Threshold
parameters.description: alphathreshold - This value is what the pixel's alpha is compared to to determine if the pixel should be drawn. Pixels with alpha greater than the Alpha Threshold will be drawn. Pixels with alpha less than or equal to the Alpha Threshold will not be drawn.

parameters.name: Wire Frame
parameters.label: Wire Frame
parameters.description:
wireframe -  - Enables and disables wire-frame rendering with the option of OpenGL Tesselated or Topology based wireframes.

 off - tesselated - topology -

parameters.name: Line Width
parameters.label: Line Width
parameters.description: wirewidth - This value is the width that the wires will be. This value is in pixels.

parameters.name: Cull Face
parameters.label: Cull Face
parameters.description:
cullface -  - Selects which faces to render.

 userender - Use the render settings found in the Render or Render Pass . neither - Do not cull any faces, render everything. backfaces - Cull back faces, render front faces. frontfaces - Cull front faces, render back faces. bothfaces - Cull both faces, render nothing.

parameters.name: Use Render Setting
parameters.label: Use Render Setting
parameters.description: userender - Use the render settings found in the Render or Render Pass . neither - Do not cull any faces, render everything. backfaces - Cull back faces, render front faces. frontfaces - Cull front faces, render back faces. bothfaces - Cull both faces, render nothing.

parameters.name: Polygon Depth Offset
parameters.label: Polygon Depth Offset
parameters.description: polygonoffset - Turns on the polygon offset feature.

parameters.name: Offset Factor
parameters.label: Offset Factor
parameters.description: polygonoffsetfactor -

parameters.name: Offset Units
parameters.label: Offset Units
parameters.description: polygonoffsetunits -
